title: Ban a slug for the rest of the session after per-slug dollar loss cap is hit
objective: Add a per-session dollar-loss cap per slug so one toxic market can be banned before trade-count cap alone is reached.

context.problem: Count-only rules are not enough. A single slug can do outsized damage before hitting a trade count cap if losses are large or gap-driven.
evidence: CWS-KC and HOU-SEA produced repeated concentrated losses
Gap-stop clusters can create heavy damage before count-based controls fully engage

required_work: 1. Add a per-session realized-loss check per slug in the live entry gate path.
2. Reject further entries on a slug once cumulative session realized loss on that slug is at or below the configured negative dollar threshold.
4. Log rejections clearly with slug and measured realized loss.
acceptance_criteria: Further entries on a slug are blocked after the slug reaches the configured session loss cap
Rejection is logged in standard gate format
No files outside allowed scope modified
python -m py_compile core/risk.py passes if modified
verification: Show where slug loss is computed
Run py_compile on every changed file
rollback: Revert only the files listed in allowed_files if they were changed for this task.
restart_note: Restart required for the new slug-loss cap to affect live entry gating.
